title: Fermented Venezuelan Onion Grill
description: A fermented and snappy twist on traditional Venezuelan cuisine, featuring onions braised on a robata grill. This dish is a delicious and anti-inflammatory-friendly option for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat (around 400°F).

Braid the onions by cutting them into thin slices and layering them on a baking sheet. Drizzle with a little olive oil and sprinkle with brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic, salt, and black pepper. Let it sit for 30 minutes before grilling.
Place the braid on the robata grill and close the lid. Baste with olive oil every 5 minutes for 20 minutes, or until the onions are caramelized and slightly charred.
Remove the onions from the grill and let them cool slightly. Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
